design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect food and bones through side-scrolling platforming levels while defeating enemies, managing limited weapon ammo, and utilizing special abilities to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (food collection for village survival) balanced against exploration for optimal completion percentages and optional bonus content
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Stone Age hero Sam tasked with saving his village from starvation by adventuring through dinosaur-infested levels, using period-appropriate weapons and special abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unusually generous difficulty scaling philosophy (16 continues provided) combined with percentage-based level completion scoring that rewards thorough exploration without mandating 100% collection. Mix of traditional platforming with level-specific gimmicks (vehicles, key-finding, boss encounters) prevents mechanical monotony. Story-driven narrative (village survival stakes) integrated into gameplay loop via bone currency and shop systems.

difficulty.lives_system: Three-heart health system per life; losing all hearts restarts current level
difficulty.continues_system: Unusually high continue count (16 total) allowing extensive player persistence through difficulty spikes
difficulty.checkpointing: Midway checkpoint (Rees-Tartah marker) in each level prevents full restart punishment
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: shops allow purchasing level skips, game saves can be bought with bones. Percentage completion system removes binary pass/fail mentality. Optional collectibles (B-O-N-U-S letters for bonus areas) provide skill-gated rewards without blocking progression.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon variety (spiked club default, spears, axes with limited ammo); temporary invincibility shields; permanent health increase containers; special movement abilities (running 'like a dog', shout to defeat all on-screen enemies)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression via checkpoint completion. Bonus areas unlocked by collecting B-O-N-U-S letter sets. Shop purchases unlock convenience items and level skips.
replayability_hooks: Percentage completion scoring encourages replays for optimal performance
Hidden level select accessible via button combinations rewards exploration of menus
Bonus areas reward letter collection across multiple playthroughs
Different level gimmicks (vehicle segments, key-finding, boss encounters) create gameplay variety within linear structure

world_building_techniques: Opening narrative hook: Village Chief assigns Sam rescue mission due to dinosaur-caused food theft and impending winter starvation
NPCs providing hints and tutorial guidance throughout levels
Food collection mechanic ties directly to plot stakes (village survival)
Stone Age setting established through visual design and weapon types
End-of-level food tally system reinforces narrative objective

faction_indicators: Village collective (Sam's home) vs. dinosaur antagonists
Shop merchants as neutral NPCs enabling player progression
danger_signals: Greedy dinosaurs as clear antagonistic force
Winter starvation threat creating environmental pressure
Food scarcity as resource management tension
environmental_storytelling: Bone currency system reflects primitive economy and combat spoils
Shop locations as safe havens within hostile level environments
Checkpoint markers (Rees-Tartah) as progression validation points
Level-specific gimmicks signal environmental adaptation (vehicles for traversal challenges, keys for locked sections)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Prehistorik Man (SNES)
